description: Claude CLI stream showing the cancel path: AskUserQuestion is emitted, user cancels, relay calls proc.kill(), stream ends without result:success. Regression: pre-fix, proc.kill() was NOT called → proc.exited never resolved → freeze.
derived_rationale: The cancel path does not produce a result:success line. After proc.kill(), Claude CLI emits no further events. The stream terminates and proc.exited resolves. This fixture documents the expected short-circuit behavior after fix (6ce2d73 + freeze fix).
trigger: claudeStream in interactive mode; user taps '✖ Cancel' on Telegram form → form.reject(new Error('user cancelled')) → handleAskUserQuestion catch block → proc.kill()
notes: The fix: try/catch around onQuestion() in handleAskUserQuestion
On rejection, proc.kill() is called immediately — no write to stdin
claudeStream resolves with resultText='' (no result:success line emitted)
The relay catches the rejection from onQuestion and kills the process
Bug to detect: if proc.kill() is NOT called, proc.exited hangs indefinitely
Stream terminates after the AskUserQuestion tool_use line — no user event, no result
This is a truncated stream: Claude emits AskUserQuestion, then process is killed externally

cancel_event.description: Stream ends here because proc.kill() was called after user cancelled. No further lines emitted by Claude CLI.
cancel_event.trigger: form.reject(new Error('user cancelled')) → handleAskUserQuestion catch block → proc.kill()

payload.exitNote: exitCode -1 represents SIGTERM from proc.kill() — not a Claude CLI error
